title: If an AI built your strategy, do you still own the edge or are you just operating someone else's system?
author: Important_Buy626
selftext:
Been thinking about this a lot lately. There's a philosophical question underneath the practical one: if you didn't design the logic, don't fully understand every decision it makes, and couldn't replicate it yourself is it really your strategy?

I go back and forth. On one hand, traders use indicators and tools they didn't build all the time. On the other hand, there's something different about a system that generates its own rules vs. a tool that helps you execute yours.

Does this distinction matter to you in practice, or is it just output and results that count?

comments.author: Spartacus_Nakamoto
comments.body: I just want a magic machine that I feed money into and it spit out more money than I fed into it. All else is noise.

comments.author: Cute-Let-4605
comments.body: Garage in, garbage out. In the end you need to have the idea and then AI assist in the implementation and testing. It’s wishful thinking that Claude can give you a massive edge or even a profitable strategy.

comments.author: woztrades
comments.body: AI is great at executing plans you come up with, but is terrible with taste and original ideas. That being said, 99% of people I’ve met still start with “make me a trading bot that wins, make no mistakes”
